---
title: "Carlos Gino Garibotto Sandoval"
subtitle: "Quantitative Economist  |  Data Analytics  |  Retail & Telecommunications"
format:
  html:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    page-layout: article
---

```{=html}
<div style="text-align:center; margin-bottom: 1rem;">
  <span style="color:#595959; font-size:0.95rem;">
    Lima, Peru &nbsp;|&nbsp; +51 985 344 917 &nbsp;|&nbsp;
    <a href="mailto:cargingarsan@gmail.com">cargingarsan@gmail.com</a> &nbsp;|&nbsp;
    <a href="https://cargingarsan.github.io">cargingarsan.github.io</a>
  </span>
</div>
<div style="text-align:center; margin-bottom: 2rem;">
  <a href="CV_Garibotto_EN_ATS.pdf" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">
    ⬇ Download CV (PDF)
  </a>
</div>
```

---

## Professional Profile

Economist (UNMSM) with 25+ years of experience in quantitative analysis applied to business decisions. Specialist in customer segmentation, conversion propensity models, and RFM analytics for telecommunications and retail client bases. Hands-on experience with machine learning in R (tidymodels, LightGBM, XGBoost, K-Means), deployment of data applications (Shiny, Docker, shinyapps.io), and reproducible executive reporting with Quarto. Founder of G2S Analytics. Winner of IBM Coding Day Hackathon (MIDIS, 2018).

---

## Key Competencies

| Analytics & Modeling | Tools & Platforms |
|---|---|
| Customer segmentation (K-Means, RFM analytics) | R / RStudio / tidymodels / tidyclust |
| Predictive modeling (LightGBM, XGBoost, RF) | Machine learning for business applications |
| Churn analysis and conversion modeling | Feature engineering and variable selection |
| SMOTE and imbalanced class handling | SHAP values and model interpretability |
| Shiny Apps and executive dashboards | Docker · shinyapps.io · GitHub Pages |
| Quarto: reproducible reports | Power BI (Microsoft Certification) |
| SQL and relational databases | Model Context Protocol (MCP) / AI Agents |
| Time series analysis | Applied econometrics and explanatory models |

---

## Data Analytics Projects

### Digital Profile Segmentation — Entel Empresas `2025`
*Datathon Entel 2022*

- Integrated 7 data sources (~500,000 mobile lines) with 40+ engineered features
- K-Means segmentation (K=4) by app consumption profile: streaming, social media, banking and messaging
- Conversion propensity models per segment: LightGBM, XGBoost and Random Forest with SMOTE
- RFM model adapted to Peruvian prepaid market: Recency, Frequency, Monetary (GB×S/8)
- Shiny app with RFM threshold simulator deployed on shinyapps.io and Docker

[View report](segmentacion-telco/index.html) · [Interactive RFM App](https://av3aks-c0gino-garibotto0s0.shinyapps.io/rfm-telco-entel/)

---

### Digital Convergence — Propensity Model `2024`
*Datathon Entel 2022*

- LightGBM model to predict convergent product adoption in a base of ~224,000 customers
- **AUC: 0.81** | **Lift of 32.8x** in the highest-score decile | F1-Score optimized by threshold
- Top 20% by score captures 57% of all real converters
- Interactive Shiny app for score exploration and lift curve — deployed on Docker

[View report](entel/index.html)

---

### COVID-19 Peru: Temporal Lags and Regional Mortality `2024`
*G2S Analytics*

- Regression models with lagged variables (lags 0–40 days) on MINSA data across 25 departments
- Identified optimal lag between confirmed cases and fatalities
- Published on GitHub Pages with static visualizations in ggplot2

[View report](casos_vs_fallecidos_actualizado_3.html)

---

### G2S Territorial Insights — Municipal Indicators Peru `2024–Present`
*G2S Analytics*

- Integrated official sources (INEI, SIAF, SUSALUD, RENIEC) for 1,874 Peruvian districts
- Standardized socioeconomic indicator system for territorial diagnosis and investment prioritization

[View analysis](territorial_insights.html)

---

## Professional Experience

### Founder & Lead Analyst · G2S Analytics `2023 – Present · Lima, Peru`

- Developed analytical models and indicator systems for public organizations and proprietary research
- Built reproducible data pipelines in R published via GitHub Pages
- Applied machine learning to business and public policy problems

---

### Economic Research Analyst · Banco del Caribe `Jun. 1998 – Apr. 1999 · Caracas, Venezuela`
*Planning & Management Division*

- Produced macroeconomic studies and reports for the Institutional Planning Division
- Analyzed financial and economic indicators to support executive decision-making

---

### Editor · Editora Perú S.A. `May. 1994 – Sep. 1995 · Lima, Peru`
*Empresa Peruana de Servicios Editoriales*

- Editorial production at a public company in the state communications sector

---

## Education

**Bachelor's Degree in Economics** · Specialization: Business Management `2023`\
Universidad Nacional Mayor de San Marcos (UNMSM) — School of Economics · Lima, Peru

---

## Certifications & Awards

- 🏆 **Winner** — IBM Coding Day Hackathon #SinAnemia · IBM Peru / MIDIS `2018`
- Dashboard in a Day — Power BI | SQL PASS Lima & Power BI User Group Lima `2017`
- PowerBI Official Workshop — BIExpert / Microsoft `2017`

---

## Languages

| Language | Level |
|---|---|
| Spanish | Native |
| English | Intermediate — advanced technical reading |
